span.p: Same large context re-sent across requests?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Lever 1: Prompt caching
cache the stable prefix - biggest, lowest-risk win

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Every call on the flagship model?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Lever 2: Routing ladder
Haiku triage -> Sonnet -> Opus on the hard tail; measure cost-per-resolved-task

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Lever 3: Batch API
~50% off - don't pay interactive rates for offline work

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Outputs / max_tokens larger than the task needs?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Lever 4: Right-size max_tokens + trim context
cap output; drop tokens that don't earn their place

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Re-measure cost-per-resolved-task
if still high, the task may genuinely need the spend - eval, don't guess
